nonBlockingIssues.description: Redundant build_parent_map + abs_bounds computation in partial update path. The PREV_BOUNDS update for changed nodes could be folded into the damage computation loop to avoid a redundant O(N) parent map build.
Lossy i32-to-i16 cast for coordinates uses .max(0) but no .min(i16::MAX as i32) guard. Not a correctness issue for current framebuffer sizes but a minor defensive improvement.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add to Architecture Context: 'abs_bounds assumes scroll_y=0 for all nodes during damage rect computation. Core must pre-apply scroll to viewport-relative coordinates before publishing the scene. If a future change sets scroll_y != 0 for incremental updates, abs_bounds (and compositor damage via PREV_BOUNDS) would produce incorrect results.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: scene/lib.rs abs_bounds() walks parent chain summing x,y but ignores scroll_y. core/scene_state.rs pre-applies scroll. populate_prev_bounds() also uses abs_bounds. This implicit coupling was flagged in both round 1 and round 2 reviews.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add to Testing & Validation Guidance: 'For damage-tracking changes, verify cursor-only moves (arrow keys) and node movement in general do not leave ghost artifacts. Damage tracking must dirty both old AND new positions of moved nodes.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The original compositor-change-list-damage feature's visual verification only tested typing and clock updates but not arrow-key cursor movement. The old-position ghost regression was not caught until scrutiny review. The fix worker then properly verified with arrow keys in both directions.

rejectedObservations.observation: PREV_BOUNDS uses compact (i16, i16, u16, u16) = 4 KiB instead of the originally suggested 8 KiB [i32;4]. Could be noted as an established pattern for per-node metadata in compositor statics.
rejectedObservations.reason: Too granular for library entry — this is a single implementation detail visible directly in the code.
